sourceMovie.title: Groundhog Day
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Groundhog Day (1993) as a romance theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The romance and fantasy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: When We First Met
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Groundhog Day clicked for you, When We First Met (2018) is a strong next stop: both trade in romance and fantasy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Groundhog Day?
faq.answer: Try When We First Met, A Midsummer Night's Dream, Hotel Transylvania 3: Summer Vacation—each shares DNA with Groundhog Day while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Groundhog Day?
faq.answer: Groundhog Day (1993) is best described as Romance, Fantasy, Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Groundhog Day worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this romance lane.
